data.title: Venu Holding GAAP EPS of -$0.48, revenue of $3.5M

data.title: Horizon Technology Finance announces CEO transition plan
data.description:
• Horizon Technology Finance (NASDAQ:HRZN) announced that, as part of HRZN’s succession planning, Robert D. Pomeroy, Jr. will retire as chief executive officer of the company.
  • Gerald A. Michaud will retire as president, both effective June 5, 2025.
  • Board has appointed Michael P. Balkin to succeed Pomeroy as CEO.
  • Pomeroy will remain chairman of the board after his retirement.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway exits Citi, cuts BofA, COF stakes, among other Q1 moves
data.description:
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) (BRK.A) continued to lighten its holdings of banks and loaded up on Constellation Brands (STZ) in Q1 2025, according to its latest 13F filing.

The insurance company with a hefty securities portfolio divested the rest of its 14.6M shares in Citigroup (C) and 40.2M shares of Nubank (NU).

It reduced its holdings in Bank of America (BAC) to 631.6M shares ($26.4B) from 680.2M, Capital One Financial (COF) to 7.15M shares ($1.28B) from 7.45M, and DaVita (DVA) to 35.1M shares ($5.38B) from 36.1M, among other moves.

Increased stakes included: Constellation Brands (STZ) to 12.0M shares, valued at $2.2B at March 31, 2025, from M shares; VeriSign to 13.3M shares ($3.37B) from M shares; and Pool Corp. (POOL) to 1.46M shares ($466.1M) from M shares. 

Notably, Berkshire (NYSE:BRK.B) held its Apple (AAPL) stake steady at 300M shares ($66.6B) during the quarter, according to the filing.

data.title: MDU Resources Group appoints new board chair
data.description:
• MDU Resources Group (NYSE:MDU) has elected Darrel T. Anderson as its new independent chair of the board, succeeding Dennis W. Johnson, effective immediately.
  • Johnson will remain on the board as a director and will continue to serve as chair of the Nominating and Governance committee.
  • Anderson joined the board in 2023, and previously served as board vice chair and chair of the Compensation and Human Capital committee.  
  • Source: Press release

  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities. 
  • Filing.

data.title: SA Asks: Would Trump's drug price cuts hurt biotech more than pharma?
data.description:
Would the cost-cutting measures for U.S. prescription drugs proposed by the Trump administration on Monday hurt the biotech sector more than pharma?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Stephen Ayers and Edmund Ingham for their views.

Stephen Ayers: Pharmaceutical companies are more directly impacted by the proposed cuts, but they have the resources to withstand some pressure. The impact on biotechnology companies is more nuanced, but these changes will be reflected in valuations. If the pricing power is reduced across the board for drugs, a Phase 2 investigational drug’s net present value, or NPV, for example, would be lower than in the past, reducing the valuation of a biotechnology company.  

Edmund Ingham: Right now, the proposed cuts to drug prices have - somewhat bizarrely - brought buoyancy to biotech and pharma stocks - the biotech ETF (NYSEARCA:XBI) gained >4% on Monday, as did stock prices of most Big Pharma companies.  

This may reflect one of two things - firstly, that pharmas and biotechs are not taking the president seriously - after all, these same proposed policy changes were easily defeated in President Trump’s first term in office by the pharma industry. Secondly, the biotech sector is suffering, and has been for some time. Having hit rock bottom, perhaps the industry is prepared to welcome change of any kind. 

  • Top Pharmaceuticals Stocks
  • Top Biotechnology Stocks

data.title: Janus International Group increases share buyback program by $75M
data.description:
• Janus International Group (NYSE:JBI) has expanded JBI’s existing share repurchase program to purchase up to an additional $75M of its common stock.
  • The prior authorization was for $100M.
  • As of March 29, 2025, JBI has repurchased ~7.8M shares of common stock at $83.7M.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Globus Medical announces share buyback program of up to $500M
data.description:
• Globus Medical (NYSE:GMED) has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $500M of GMED’s outstanding common stock.
  • GMED shares up over 1% post-market on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: HHS to drop routine COVID shots recommendation for children, pregnant women - WSJ
data.description:
The Department of Health and Human Services is expected to drop recommendations for routine COVID-19 vaccination for pregnant women, teenagers and children, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday.

This news comes amidst FDA Commissioner Marty Makary's announcement that the agency is preparing a new regulatory framework clarifying its guidance related to vaccines, particularly COVID-19 shots.

According to the WSJ report citing people familiar with the matter, the HHS is expected to remove the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's (CDC) recommendations for the specified groups at the same time it also launches a new vaccine framework.

As per the current recommendation by the CDC, which is a part of the HHS, everyone six months and older, including pregnant women, should receive Covid-19 vaccines.

The Trump administration's recent hires to the health department, including HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., have been critical of the COVID-19 vaccine shots with messenger-RNA technology.

Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), Biontech (NASDAQ:BNTX) and Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX) are among some big names with COVID-19 vaccine authorizations.

The exact date of this announcement is yet to be confirmed, but it is expected in the upcoming days, the report added.

data.title: Arena Group GAAP EPS of $0.08, revenue of $31.8M
data.description:
